questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Power factor correction circuit:
options: Shapes input current to follow input voltage
questions.solution: PFC: makes input appear resistive. Boost converter in CCM. Required by IEC 61000-3-2 for harmonic limits.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Z-transform of causal unit step u[n]:
questions.solution: Z{u[n]} = Σ z^-n from 0 to ∞ = 1/(1-z^-1), ROC: |z|>1 (causal).

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: TEM wave in coaxial cable: E and H fields are:
questions.solution: TEM: both E and H purely transverse. No field component in propagation direction. Unique to two-conductor lines.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: RTOS context switch: saves/restores:
questions.solution: Context switch: save current task's registers (PC, SP, general regs), load next task's. OS manages save area.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Kelvin connection: eliminates error due to:
questions.solution: Kelvin (4-wire): separate current and voltage leads. Voltage measured across DUT, not including lead drops.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 100 V is modulated by a 20 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 2-to-4 decoder outputs are:
options: One-hot (only one active at a time)
questions.solution: Decoder: n inputs → 2ⁿ outputs. Exactly one output active (high).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Characteristic equation s³+2s²+s+K=0. For marginal stability using Routh:
questions.solution: Routh: s³:1,1; s²:2,K; s¹:(2-K)/2; s⁰:K. For marginal: (2-K)/2=0→K=2. Aux: 2s²+2=0→s=±j1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 15 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Resonant converter: ZVS achieved when:
options: Switch turns on when voltage across it is zero
questions.solution: ZVS: voltage across switch reaches zero before turn-on. Reduces switching loss. Important for high-frequency converters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If a 4 kHz signal is sampled at 6 kHz, the result is:
options: Aliasing (below the Nyquist rate of 8 kHz)
questions.solution: Nyquist rate = 2×4 = 8 kHz; sampling at 6 kHz under-samples and aliases.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Single-phase full bridge inverter output frequency controlled by:
questions.solution: Inverter frequency = switching frequency. Square wave or PWM. PWM allows harmonic control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 3 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Three-phase VSI: line-to-line RMS voltage:
questions.solution: SPWM: VLL(rms) = (√3/2√2) × Vdc ≈ 0.612 × Vdc. With overmodulation or SVPWM, can reach 0.707.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: System described by y[n]-0.5y[n-1]=x[n]+0.3x[n-1] is:
questions.solution: Has feedback term y[n-1] → IIR (infinite impulse response). FIR has no feedback.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gain margin:
questions.solution: GM: how much gain can increase before instability. GM > 0 dB for stable system.

questions.stem: A BJT with β = 100 has a base current of 40 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root locus — asymptotes:
questions.solution: Asymptotes: meet at centroid. For n-m = 1: -180°. n-m = 2: ±90°. n-m = 3: ±60°, 180°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LED emits light when it is:
questions.solution: Forward-biased recombination of carriers releases photons in an LED.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bandwidth of closed-loop system is typically:
questions.solution: Closed-loop bandwidth > open-loop (faster response but more noise).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=100kΩ, Rin=2.2kΩ, Vin=0.5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The built-in potential of a silicon diode is:
questions.solution: Si: ~0.7 V. Ge: ~0.3 V. GaAs: ~1.2 V. V_bi = (kT/q)·ln(N_A N_D/n_i²).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=22kΩ, R₁=22kΩ. Closed-loop gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many bits are needed to encode 8 distinct quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The directivity (gain) of an antenna increases as its:
options: Effective aperture increases relative to wavelength
questions.solution: Gain ∝ 4π·A_eff/λ²; larger apertures (in wavelengths) give higher directivity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Race-around condition in JK flip-flop occurs when:
options: J=K=1 and clock pulse is too long
questions.solution: Race-around: output toggles multiple times per clock if J=K=1 and clock too long. Master-slave avoids this.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transmission line characteristic impedance Z₀ = √(L/C). For L=0.5μH/m, C=50pF/m:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Orthogonality of basis functions φ₁, φ₂: ∫φ₁(t)φ₂(t)dt=0 means:
options: No projection of one onto the other
questions.solution: Orthogonal: inner product zero. Energy in each basis is independent. Used in Fourier, wavelet analysis.

questions.stem: LTI system output y[n] = x[n] * h[n]. If x[n]={1,2,3} and h[n]={1,1}:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lag compensator:
options: Adds phase lag. Improves steady-state accuracy. Pole before zero. Reduces BW
questions.solution: Lag: high low-f gain → lower SSE. Reduces BW → slower response. α = Tz/Tp > 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flip-flop — T:
options: Toggles on each clock edge. T=1 → Q' = Q'. T=0 → hold
questions.solution: T: from JK (J=K=T). Used in counters. Divide-by-2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Frequency modulation (FM):
options: Carrier frequency varies with modulating signal amplitude
questions.solution: FM: s(t) = A_c·cos(ω_c t + β·sin(ω_m t)). β = Δf/f_m = modulation index.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Threshold effect in FM occurs when:
questions.solution: FM threshold: below ~10 dB SNR, output SNR degrades rapidly. FM improvement fails.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DSB-SC modulation:
options: Suppressed carrier. No carrier power. Requires coherent detection
questions.solution: DSB-SC: V(t) = m(t)·cos(ωct). Synchronous (coherent) detection required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM with m=0.3, input SNR=10. Output SNR (envelope detector):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple access — TDMA:
options: Each user assigned time slot. Digital. GSM: 8 users per 200 kHz carrier
questions.solution: TDMA: framing. GSM: 8 time slots × 114 bits each = 577 μs burst. Adaptive equalization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 5 kHz and message bandwidth 10 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 50 V is modulated by a 30 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel capacity: B=3kHz, SNR=30dB. Shannon limit ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nyquist sampling rate:
questions.solution: Nyquist: f_s ≥ 2f_max to avoid aliasing. Aliasing = high frequencies fold into baseband.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A channel of bandwidth 3 kHz has an SNR of 20 dB. Its Shannon capacity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nyquist criterion for zero ISI:
options: Pulse shape: sinc function. Roll-off factor α (0 to 1). BW = (1+α)R_b/2
questions.solution: Raised cosine: α=0 (ideal sinc, infinite tail), α=1 (full cosine roll-off, 2× BW).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quantization error in PCM:
options: ±Δ/2 (Δ = step size). Decreases with more quantization levels
questions.solution: SQNR = 6.02n + 1.76 dB (n = bits per sample). Each additional bit → +6 dB SQNR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM modulation index:
options: β = Δf/f_m (peak deviation / modulating frequency). Narrowband β<0.2, Wideband β>1
questions.solution: FM: β determines bandwidth. Carson's rule: BW = 2(Δf + f_m). NBFM: BW ≈ 2f_m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM signal power depends on:
questions.solution: FM: power = A_c²/2. Independent of β. Same as unmodulated carrier.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Matched filter:
options: Optimal detector for AWGN. h(t) = s(T-t). Maximizes SNR at sampling instant
questions.solution: Matched filter: impulse response = time-reversed signal. SNR_max = 2E/N0.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: DSSS synchronization: acquisition stage finds:
questions.solution: Acquisition: coarse alignment of spreading code. Tracking: fine alignment and maintenance. Uses correlators.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Antilog (exponential) amplifier:
options: V_out ∝ exp(V_in). Uses BJT in feedback. V_out = I_S·R·exp(V_in/V_T)
questions.solution: Antilog: exponential function. Used in analog computation. Multiplier: log+antilog.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Polar codes:
options: Channel polarization. Arıkan. First capacity-achieving code. 5G NR control channel
questions.solution: Polar: binary expansion. N = 2^n. Frozen bits. SC/SCL decoding. Systematic polar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Window comparator:
options: Detects if input between two thresholds. Two comparators + AND gate
questions.solution: Window: V_in between V_L and V_H → output high. Battery monitoring, limit detection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Active filter — Butterworth:
options: Maximally flat passband. Roll-off: -20n dB/decade (n = order)
questions.solution: Butterworth: no ripple. Chebyshev: ripple in passband, sharper cutoff. Elliptic: ripple in both.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Active band-pass filter center frequency for Sallen-Key topology:
questions.solution: Sallen-Key: f₀ = 1/(2π√(R₁R₂C₁C₂)). Q-factor depends on component ratios.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Barkhausen criterion for oscillation:
questions.solution: Loop gain = 1 at 0° phase. For start-up: |Aβ| > 1. Steady-state: |Aβ| = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: State-space: observability matrix O = [C; CA; CA^2; ...; CA^(n-1)]. System is observable if:
questions.solution: Observability: can all states be determined from output? Full rank = yes. Kalman test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phase shift oscillator requires 180° phase shift from 3 RC sections. Each section provides:
questions.solution: 3 RC sections: each 60° at oscillation frequency. Total 180°. Op-amp provides additional 180°.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: LMS algorithm: convergence speed depends on:
options: Step size and input signal eigenvalues
questions.solution: Convergence: μ controls speed/stability tradeoff. Eigenvalue spread of input autocorrelation affects convergence rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ideal op-amp has:
options: Infinite input impedance, zero output impedance, infinite gain
questions.solution: Ideal op-amp: Z_in = ∞, Z_out = 0, A_OL = ∞, BW = ∞.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Difference amplifier output: Vout = (Rf/R₁)(V₂-V₁) when all R equal:
questions.solution: Difference amp: V_out = (Rf/R₁)(V₂-V₁). For all equal R: V₂-V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region of convergence (ROC) of the Laplace transform determines:
options: Whether the system is stable and/or causal
questions.solution: Stability requires the ROC to include the jω-axis; ROC position also encodes causality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Colpitts oscillator:
options: LC oscillator. Capacitive divider (C1, C2). f = 1/(2π√(L·C1C2/(C1+C2)))
questions.solution: Colpitts: tapped capacitor. Hartley: tapped inductor. Crystal: most stable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wave impedance of plane wave in lossy medium:
questions.solution: Lossy: Z is complex. sigma/(omega*eps) >> 1: good conductor. << 1: good dielectric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiplexing — TDM:
options: Time Division. Each channel gets time slot. Synchronous (fixed) or statistical
questions.solution: TDM: digital. PSTN (E1/T1). Statistical TDM: variable slot allocation. Frame synchronization.
